https://www.ancestry.com/interactive/2495/40400_274570-00409/46568318?backurl=http%3A//person.ancestry.com/tree/90242443/person/270005589718/facts/citation/840030451866/edit/record#?imageId=40400_274570-00409

Origin Displayed: Scottish - Spelling variations of this family name include: Huggins, Huggan, Hugan, Huckings, Huckins, Huggin and others. First found in Fifeshire where they were seated from very ancient times, some say well before the Norman Conquest and the arrival of Duke William at Hastings in 1066 C.E. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Nicholas Huggins settled in Virginia in 1635; Roger Huggins settled in New Jersey in 1678; George Huggins settled in North Carolina in 1701; Humphrey Huggins settled in New England in 1684. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). This most interesting surname is a patronymic form of "Huggin", which itself is a diminutive from the Old French personal name "Hugh", which was introduced into England by the Normans after the Conquest of 1066. Hugh is a contracted form of any of the various Germanic compound names with the first element "hug", heart, mind, spirit. This personal name was popular among the Normans in England, due to the fame of St. Hugh of Lincoln (1140 - 1200), who established the first Carthusian monastery in England. Thus the surname is composed of "Hug", the pet form of Hugh; the diminutive suffix "-in"; and the patronymic ending "-s", son of; hence "son of little Hugh". The surname is first recorded in the early 14th Century, while other early examples include: Amisia Hugines, recorded in the Subsidy Rolls of Worcestershire in 1327; and John Hugyn, mentioned in the Feet of Fines of Staffordshire in 1337. Samuel Huggins (1811 - 1885), was President of the Liverpool Architectural Society, and the Society for the Protection of Ancient Buildings was founded largely due to his papers against "restorations" of cathedrals. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Robert Hugyn, which was dated 1327, in the "Subsidy Rolls of Sussex", during the reign of King Edward 3rd, known as "The Father of the Navy", 1327 - 1377. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Huckins於1649年8月2日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波時，他的父親Edward Sturgis32歲，母親Rose33歲。他於1670年8月10日與Hope Chipman在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波結婚。他們至少是5個女兒的父母。他去世於1678年11月10日在他的家鄉享年29歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波巴恩斯塔波。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Huckins于1649年8月2日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波时，他的父亲Edward Sturgis32岁，母亲Rose33岁。他于1670年8月10日与Hope Chipman在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波结婚。他们至少是5个女儿的父母。他去世于1678年11月10日在他的家乡享年29岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波巴恩斯塔波。

nameMeanings.name: Huckins
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Oxfordshire): from a pet form of the personal name Hew or How, with post-medieval excrescent -s (see Hugh ).

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John Huckins was born on 2 August 1649, in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Edward Sturgis, was 32 and his mother, Rose, was 33. He married Hope Chipman on 10 August 1670, in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 daughters. He died on 10 November 1678, in his hometown, at the age of 29, and was buried in Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Huckins est né le 2 août 1649, à Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Edward Sturgis, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Rose, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Hope Chipman le 10 août 1670, à Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 filles. Il est décédé le 10 novembre 1678, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 29 ans, et a été enterré à Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Huckins è nato il il 2 agosto 1649 nel Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Edward Sturgis, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Rose, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Hope Chipman il 10 agosto 1670, nel Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figlie. Morì il 10 novembre 1678, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 29 anni, e fu sepolto a Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: John Huckinsが1649年8月2日にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーバーンスタブルで生まれた時，父Edward Sturgisは32歳，母Roseは33歳であった。1670年8月10日にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーバーンスタブルでHope Chipmanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の娘の親であった。彼は1678年11月10日に故郷で29歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーバーンスタブルバーンスタブルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Huckins(이)가 1649년 8월 2일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Sturgis(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Rose(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1670년 8월 10일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일에서 Hope Chipman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1678년 11월 10일 그의 고향에서 29세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 바른스타블일에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Huckins nasceu em 2 de agosto de 1649, em Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, Edward Sturgis, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Rose, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Hope Chipman em 10 de agosto de 1670, em Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de novembro de 1678, em sua cidade natal, com 29 anos, e foi sepultado em Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Huckins родился 2 августа 1649 г. в местности барнстабл, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Edward Sturgis было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Rose, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Hope Chipman 10 августа 1670 г. в местности барнстабл, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 дочерей. Он умер 10 ноября 1678 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 29 лет и был похоронен в местности барнстабл, барнстабл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Huckins nació el 2 de agosto de 1649, en Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Edward Sturgis, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Rose, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Hope Chipman el 10 de agosto de 1670, en Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijas. Murió el 10 de noviembre de 1678, en su pueblo, a la edad de 29 años, y fue sepultado en Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
